name: HMS Brave (J 305)
events: 16 Sep 1943 HMS H 50 (Lt. J.C. Varley, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Brave (T/A/Lt.Cdr. F.S. Tolliday, RNR). (1)
8 Jan 1944 Around 1000 A/8, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) departed Malta for Beirut where she was to join the 1st Submarine Flotilla.

She was routes as follows; Through position 35°49'N, 15°06'E at 1358 A/8 thence proceeding independently on the surface to position 34°43'N, 20°02'E at 2001 A/9 thence diving by day to position 34°15'N, 25°01'E at 2010 A/11th for onward routeing to Beirut which will be signalled by the Capt. S.1. Speed of advance, 200 miles every 24 hours when on the surface and 120 miles every 24 hours when dived by day.

In a signal timed 1106 B/8 the Capt. S.8 sent the following orders; From position 34°15'N, 25°01'E proceed on surface at 8 knots through position 34°05'N, 29°59'E at 0232 B/13 to arrive at Beirut at 1031 B/14.
She was escorted out until 1313 A/8 by HMS Brave (T/A/Lt.Cdr. F.S. Tolliday, RNR). (2)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
